story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is expected to hold interest rates at its June meeting, with multiple sources indicating a status quo decision. Economists suggest the RBI may maintain this stance while forecasting potential rate hikes twice in FY27. Additionally, concerns over rupee weakness are not seen as a decisive factor for a rate increase at this meeting.

story_clusters.summary: The sources agree that the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is unlikely to raise interest rates, with inflation being a key focus. Gita Gopinath and SBI Chairman Dinesh Kumar Khara both suggest that the rupee’s depreciation should act as a shock absorber rather than prompting aggressive RBI intervention. Khara also emphasizes that India’s economic growth extends beyond stock market performance.

story_clusters.summary: A fire at a guest house in Delhi's Malviya Nagar killed 21 people, including foreigners, and injured over 40 others. Authorities have arrested the building owner, Lavkesh Bajaj, and are investigating a manager who allegedly operated the property without a No Objection Certificate (NoC) from the Delhi Fire Department. An FIR has been filed, and the government has stated that those responsible will be held accountable.

articles.summary: The Telangana Advocates Protection Act 2026 officially came into force on June 2, 2026, two months after being passed by the state legislative assembly. The government issued a notification appointing June 2 as the date for the Act's provisions to take effect. The law aims to protect advocates in the state, though specific provisions are not detailed in the article.

articles.title: Remove The Predator, Not The Prey
articles.summary: The article examines Section 12 of India's Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013, which provides interim relief during workplace harassment inquiries. It critiques how institutions have applied this provision over the past 12 years and suggests reforms to better protect women while ensuring fair processes. The discussion focuses on balancing interim relief for complainants with the rights of the accused during pending investigations.
articles.raw_summary: Rethinking Interim Relief Under Section 12 of the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 I. The Structural Paradox The Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013 (' the POSH Act ') was enacted to protect women at their workplaces. articles.title: Section 115BAA vs Normal Tax Regime: A Comparative Analysis for Tax Planning
articles.summary: The article compares Section 115BAA, a concessional tax regime for eligible domestic companies in India, with the normal tax structure. It highlights that Section 115BAA offers a fixed lower tax rate but restricts certain deductions available under the standard regime. The analysis is aimed at helping companies with tax planning under the existing policy framework.
articles.raw_summary: Section 115BAA offers eligible domestic companies a concessional tax structure with a fixed effective rate. The trade-off is the loss of several deductions available under the normal regime. The post Section 115BAA vs Normal Tax Regime: A Comparative Analysis for Tax Planning appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: How MCA’s ZCZP Recognition Links CSR with Social Stock Exchange
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has amended rules to allow companies to use a portion of their Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) funds for Zero Coupon Zero Principal (ZCZP) instruments listed on the Social Stock Exchange. This creates a new regulated channel for deploying CSR funds, aligning corporate social impact with financial instruments. The move aims to enhance transparency and efficiency in CSR spending while leveraging market mechanisms for social good.
articles.raw_summary: New MCA amendments permit companies to deploy a portion of CSR funds through Zero Coupon Zero Principal instruments listed on the Social Stock Exchange, creating an additional regulated implementation channel. The post How MCA’s ZCZP Recognition Links CSR with Social Stock Exchange appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Commercial Dispute Resolution: Corporate Lawyers' Evolving Role In Repositioning First Forums
articles.summary: The article traces the historical shift in commercial dispute resolution from informal negotiations to formal state-regulated adjudication. It highlights how globalization and legal formalization have transformed trust-based systems into enforceable rights and procedures. The discussion focuses on the evolving role of corporate lawyers in repositioning dispute resolution forums, which may have implications for India's legal and regulatory landscape.
articles.raw_summary: Trade and commerce are as old as civilization itself, and so are the conflicts that accompany them. For most of history, commercial disputes were settled around tables through negotiation, trust, and mutual dependence. As trade expanded and transactions grew more complex, informal dialogue-based resolution gave way to state-regulated adjudication. articles.title: White Paper on Kerala finances calls for KIIFB overhaul, forensic audit of accounts
articles.summary: A white paper on Kerala's finances has recommended overhauling the Kerala Infrastructure Investment Fund Board (KIIFB) and conducting a forensic audit of its accounts. The document acknowledges KIIFB's past contributions but calls the current framework wasteful. The recommendations come from an official state document, highlighting potential governance and financial policy implications for Kerala.

articles.title: CBIC Bars Direct DGFT References to Ensure Uniform FTP Interpretation
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has instructed Customs officials not to seek clarifications directly from the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) regarding the Foreign Trade Policy (FTP). The new mechanism aims to ensure consistent interpretation and faster decision-making in trade policy implementation. This directive is intended to streamline trade policy enforcement and reduce inconsistencies in its application.
articles.raw_summary: CBIC has directed Customs field formations not to seek FTP clarifications directly from DGFT. The new mechanism aims to ensure consistency, faster decision-making, and uniform implementation of trade policy provisions. articles.title: Govt assesses measures to protect MSMEs from West Asia crisis impact
articles.summary: The government reviewed potential risks to Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) due to the West Asia crisis, focusing on raw material availability, prices, exports, imports, gas supplies, transportation, logistics, and working capital. The Ministry assessed these challenges during a review meeting to understand their impact on MSMEs. The assessment highlights concerns over supply chain disruptions and cost pressures that could affect India's trade and industrial sectors.

articles.title: DGFT Expands QCO Exemption for SEZ Imports to All Authorized Operational Goods
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has expanded exemptions for Special Economic Zone (SEZ) units and developers, allowing imports of all permissible goods required for authorized operations. Compliance with Quality Control Orders (QCO) and Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) norms will only apply when goods are cleared into the Domestic Tariff Area. This move aims to facilitate smoother operations for SEZ entities while maintaining regulatory oversight.
articles.raw_summary: DGFT has widened QCO/BIS exemptions for SEZ Units and Developers by allowing imports of all permissible goods required for authorized operations. articles.title: Maharashtra State Commission: Pre-Printed Consent Form Without Disclosure Of Surgical Risks Is Not Valid Informed Consent; Doctor Liable For Deficiency In Service
articles.summary: The Maharashtra State Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ruled that an ophthalmic surgeon obtained invalid informed consent by using a pre-printed form without disclosing surgical risks. This ruling underscores the legal and policy importance of proper disclosure in medical procedures to protect patient rights.

articles.title: Reversal of ITC against Retention Money of Supplier
articles.summary: The article argues that retention money withheld by buyers from suppliers under agreed contract terms should not be treated as non-payment under Section 16(2) of the GST law. It states that since the payment is not yet due, Input Tax Credit (ITC) should not be denied to suppliers. The discussion is relevant to tax policy and contractual practices in India's GST framework.

The verdict underscores the importance of fair procedure in administrative actions.

articles.summary: The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has amended import rules for specified silver products, now requiring a valid Import Authorisation for eligible imports. The change applies to nominated agencies, banks, and qualified jewellers importing through the India International Bullion Exchange (IIBX). This move aims to regulate silver imports more strictly. The policy was implemented by DGFT, a regulatory body under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry.

articles.title: Legal Digest | Reassessment of income is invalid without any new material evidence
articles.summary: A recent Supreme Court ruling clarifies that income tax reassessment cannot be initiated solely based on a change of opinion by tax authorities. However, if new evidence emerges showing escaped income or incorrect relief granted, reassessment is permissible even if the taxpayer had disclosed all material facts. Chartered Accountant S. Murlidharan analyzes the ruling's implications for tax administration and taxpayer rights.
articles.raw_summary: Reassessment cannot be initiated merely on a change of opinion in the absence of new material; however, where concrete, new, or previously unexamined evidence indicates that income has escaped assessment, the power to reassess may be exercised even if the assessee disclosed all material facts, provided the income was under-assessed or relief was wrongly granted, according to Chartered Accountant S. Murlidharan's analysis of a recent Supreme Court order.

articles.title: Pune ITAT: Cash Deposits Cannot Be Taxed as Unexplained Money When Scrap Business Turnover Is Accepted in Other Years
articles.summary: The Pune Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that cash deposits cannot be automatically taxed as unexplained income if the assessee's scrap business turnover has been accepted in other years. The Revenue had previously accepted the assessee's scrap business by estimating profits on turnover, and the ITAT held that this history must be considered before treating deposits as unexplained income. The ruling impacts how tax authorities assess unexplained cash deposits in cases involving scrap

articles.title: Kerala HC Allows ITC Claim as Section 16(5) Overrides Section 16(4) Time Limit
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court ruled that Section 16(5) of the GST law takes precedence over Section 16(4), allowing a taxpayer to claim Input Tax Credit (ITC) even after the prescribed deadline. The court set aside the denial of ITC since the taxpayer had filed returns before the cut-off date. This decision clarifies the legal interpretation of GST provisions and impacts how tax authorities process ITC claims. The ruling is significant for businesses and tax compliance under India's GST framework.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the non-obstante clause in Section 16(5) prevails over Section 16(4). Since the returns were filed before the prescribed cut-off date, the ITC denial was set aside. articles.summary: The Indian government has approved the widening of the Armoor-Mancherial section of NH-63 and the four-laning of the Jagtial-Karimnagar section of NH-563 in Telangana. These projects aim to reduce congestion, improve connectivity, and shorten travel times. The initiatives are expected to boost regional development and logistics under the Hybrid Annuity Model for road development.
articles.raw_summary: The government has approved significant road development projects in Telangana. The Armoor-Jagtial-Mancherial section of NH-63 will be widened. The Jagtial-Karimnagar section of NH-563 will be four-laned. These projects aim to ease congestion and improve connectivity. Travel times are expected to reduce significantly. The initiatives will boost regional development and logistics.

articles.title: Modular Kitchen Baskets Not Classifiable Under Furniture Heading as Specific Tariff Entry Prevails
articles.summary: A tribunal ruled that modular kitchen baskets, racks, and similar products cannot be classified as parts of furniture under Chapter 94 of customs tariffs, even if they are fixed in cabinets or drawers. The customs department's demand based on this classification was set aside. The ruling clarifies the specific tariff entry for these household articles, impacting import/export policies and tax treatment.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that modular kitchen baskets, racks, and similar products retain their identity as household articles and cannot be classified as parts of furniture merely because they are fixed in cabinets or drawers. The customs demand based on classification under Chapter 94 was set aside. The post Modular Kitchen Baskets Not Classifiable Under Furniture Heading as Specific Tariff Entry Prevails appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ITAT Allows Section 54F Deduction as Substantial Construction & Habitable House Were Established
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Jaipur ruled that a taxpayer could claim a deduction under Section 54F of the Income Tax Act despite delays in construction, as long as a habitable residential house was substantially built within the prescribed period. The tribunal directed the tax department to delete the addition it had made, setting a precedent for similar cases. This ruling clarifies the interpretation of Section 54F, which allows tax exemptions for investments in residential prop
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Jaipur held that deduction under Section 54F cannot be denied merely due to delay in completing construction when the assessee had substantially constructed a habitable residential house within the prescribed period. The Tribunal directed deletion of the addition. The post ITAT Allows Section 54F Deduction as Substantial Construction   Habitable House Were Established appeared first on TaxGuru .

The case raises concerns about cross-border migration, minority rights, and the lack of support for vulnerable individuals in such situations.

articles.title: Reassessment Quashed as AO Relied on Incorrect AIR Information: ITAT Amritsar
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Amritsar quashed reassessment proceedings against a taxpayer because the Assessing Officer (AO) relied on factually incorrect Annual Information Return (AIR) data without verifying bank records. The Tribunal ruled that the AO’s failure to apply proper scrutiny before issuing the notice showed a lack of due diligence. This decision highlights the importance of verifying financial records before initiating reassessment under tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Amritsar held that reassessment proceedings were invalid because the reopening was based on factually incorrect AIR information. The Tribunal ruled that failure to verify bank records before issuing notice showed non-application of mind. The post Reassessment Quashed as AO Relied on Incorrect AIR Information: ITAT Amritsar appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bogus Supplier Tag Alone Not Enough: ITAT Deletes Purchase and Loan Additions
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that labeling a supplier as 'bogus' is insufficient evidence to justify tax additions against an assessee. The tribunal emphasized that tax authorities must provide independent proof of the assessee's own transactions to sustain additions. This decision impacts how tax investigations are conducted and sets a precedent for future cases involving supplier-related tax disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that an investigation report against a supplier is only a starting point for inquiry and not conclusive proof against the assessee. The key takeaway is that additions require independent evidence relating to the assessee’s own transactions The post Bogus Supplier Tag Alone Not Enough: ITAT Deletes Purchase and Loan Additions appeared first on TaxGuru .

Additionally, 100 'Pink buses' equipped with CCTV, GPS, and panic buttons will operate exclusively for women passengers in the same cities. This initiative aims to improve urban mobility and women's safety while promoting sustainable transport. articles.title: TMC vs TMC: 59 rebel MLAs back suspended Ritabrata Banerjee as legislature party leader, party dissolves all panels
articles.summary: A faction of 59 MLAs from the Trinamool Congress (TMC) in West Bengal has backed suspended leader Ritabrata Banerjee as the legislature party leader, defying the party's decision. The TMC has dissolved all legislative panels in response. The dispute involves the anti-defection law, which requires two-thirds support (54 MLAs) to avoid disqualification for a breakaway faction.
articles.raw_summary: Under the anti-defection law, a breakaway faction requires the support of at least two-thirds of a legislature party to avoid disqualification. With the TMC having 80 MLAs in the Assembly, the threshold stands at 54.

articles.title: PhysicsWallah revises lending strategy, to partner with NBFCs for student loans
articles.summary: PhysicsWallah, an edtech company, has revised its lending strategy to partner with non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) for student loans instead of lending directly. The company will now act as a technology platform connecting students to regulated lending partners. This shift follows an earlier plan to lend through its books with FinZ infusion. The move highlights the growing role of fintech in education financing.
articles.raw_summary: In a filing on June 4, the company said it will now tie up with third-party non-banking financial companies (NBFCs) instead of lending through its books, as had been envisaged with the FinZ infusion. The edtech will henceforth work as a technology platform that connects its students to a curated list of regulated lending partners.

articles.summary: A Fast Track Court in Jammu acquitted a man accused of raping his niece, stating the prosecution case was absurd and improbable. The accused spent over a year in jail for a crime that never occurred. The court ordered a departmental inquiry against the investigating officers and directed action against the complainant under Section 182 of the Ranbir Penal Code for false prosecution. The ruling highlights issues of false cases and wrongful incarceration.

articles.title: India's Import Duties: Mills Win This Round as Farmers Absorb the Cotton Risk. Once Again

Rahman previously served as Bangladesh's National Security Adviser and High Representative on the Rohingya Issue. This election highlights Bangladesh's diplomatic role but has no immediate policy relevance for India.

articles.title: Govt trims fertilizer demand estimate after IMD downgrades monsoon forecast
articles.summary: The Indian government has reduced its estimate for fertilizer demand after the India Meteorological Department (IMD) lowered its monsoon forecast. Current fertilizer stocks stand at 19.98 million tonnes, exceeding 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, which is significantly higher than the typical 33% benchmark at this time of year. The government's adjustment reflects concerns over potential agricultural impacts due to weaker monsoon rains.
articles.raw_summary: Stocks currently stand at 19.98 million tonnes, more than 52% of the revised seasonal requirement, significantly ahead of the typical 33% benchmark at this time in the season.

articles.title: Impunity of disaster compensations
articles.summary: The article examines how disaster compensation in India is often treated as a procedural checkbox rather than a penalty for negligence, frequently punishing victims instead of addressing root causes. It critiques the systemic failure of governance in ensuring fair compensation, raising concerns about civil liberties and welfare rights of affected individuals. The piece is an editorial critique of existing processes without citing specific laws or court rulings.
articles.raw_summary: Compensation is treated not as a penalty for neglecting processes but as the process itself — one that, unfortunately, often punishes the victims

articles.title: Who is 18-year-old Sarthak Sidhant who appeared before Parliamentary Panel on CBSE OSM row?
articles.summary: An 18-year-old student, Sarthak Sidhant, appeared before a parliamentary panel to highlight alleged irregularities in a CBSE tender process. Members of Parliament questioned CBSE and the Education Ministry on vendor selection, testing, and accountability. The discussion is part of a parliamentary review of the issue.

articles.title: SC: Cops abusing authority must be dealt with sternly
articles.summary: The Supreme Court sharply criticized police officers for abusing their authority and warned that strict consequences must follow. It canceled anticipatory bail for three Maharashtra police officers accused of harassing a family and extorting money during a train search. The ruling underscores that police misconduct will not be treated with the usual leniency given to accused individuals. The decision highlights the judiciary's role in enforcing accountability within law enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court sternly stated that police officers abusing their position must face strict consequences, as normal presumptions for accused individuals won't apply. It cancelled anticipatory bail for three Maharashtra police officers accused of harassing a family and extorting money after a search on a train.

articles.title: Is apex court’s confidence in SIR exercise misplaced?

articles.title: Govt to replace WPI with Producer Price Index, new series from June 15
articles.summary: The Indian government will replace the Wholesale Price Index (WPI) with the Producer Price Index (PPI) starting June 15. This change aims to better measure price changes at the producer level. The WPI is currently used to track average price changes in a basket of commodities. The shift to PPI reflects an update in economic indicators used for policy and analysis.
articles.raw_summary: WPI is a key indicator for measuring the average change in the prices of a fixed basket of commodities at the initial stage of a commercial transaction over a given period, with reference to a base year.

articles.title: A bauxite mining project, contested consent and growing tensions
articles.summary: In April 2026, over 200 police personnel forcibly entered Kantamal village in Odisha’s Rayagada and Kalahandi districts, according to locals. The village is part of the Sijimali range, where a bauxite mining project is contested by residents. The incident highlights growing tensions over land rights and environmental concerns. Activists and villagers allege lack of proper consent and demand policy intervention.
articles.raw_summary: It was past 2:30 in the morning on April 7, 2026, when the banging on the doors began, locals of Kantamal village narrate. Kantamal is one of over 44 villages nestled in the foothills of Sijimali range in Odisha s Rayagada and Kalahandi districts. More than 200 police personnel had encircled the village, say the residents. [ ]

articles.title: OECD cuts global growth forecast, warns prolonged US-Iran war could trigger recessions
articles.summary: The OECD reduced its global growth forecast, warning that a prolonged US-Iran war in West Asia could disrupt shipping and damage energy infrastructure, slowing global growth to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027. The organization cautioned that such disruptions could have severe consequences, including potential recessions. The scenario assumes continued conflicts into 2027, affecting energy supplies and trade routes.
articles.raw_summary: OECD cautioned that a prolonged West Asia conflict would have far more severe consequences. Under a scenario where shipping disruptions and damage to energy infrastructure continue into 2027, global growth could slow to 2.1% in 2026 and 1.8% in 2027.

This initiative is expected to maintain affordable air connectivity, boost tourism, and create employment opportunities across the country.

articles.title: Changed Jobs Mid-Year? How to File ITR with Two Form 16s
articles.summary: Employees who switch jobs mid-year may face additional tax deductions at the time of filing their Income Tax Return (ITR) because each employer deducts Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) separately. This often results in a TDS shortfall rather than double taxation. The article explains how to file ITRs with two Form 16s from different employers.

Traders expect prices to remain firm in the near term.

articles.title: States asked to focus on sowing pulses, oilseeds
articles.summary: India's government is urging states to focus on pulses and oilseeds for the upcoming kharif sowing season. This directive comes amid predictions of below-normal monsoon rains and potential disruptions in fertilizer supplies. The aim is to boost domestic production and reduce reliance on imports. Efforts are underway to improve seed availability and farming techniques for these crucial crops.

Efforts are underway to improve seed availability and farming techniques for these crucial crops.

articles.title: New producer price index set to replace WPI over next five years
articles.summary: India is set to introduce a new Producer Price Index, covering output, input, and services. This move aims to provide a more accurate picture of inflation. The existing Wholesale Price Index will be phased out over five years. The new indices will use 2022-23 as the base year. This transition aligns with global standards and IMF recommendations.

The existing Wholesale Price Index will be phased out over five years. The new indices will use 2022-23 as the base year. This transition aligns with global standards and IMF recommendations.

articles.title: MSMEs to bear bigger hit than large firms from West Asia conflict: Crisil
articles.summary: The ongoing crisis in West Asia has struck a harsh chord for Indian small enterprises, leaving them vulnerable to significant setbacks. Anticipated decreases in production and revenue are creating a precarious balance for many, as mounting trade obstacles and rising expenditures apply further pressure on profits.

articles.title: India insurance market to grow 10.7% annually, outpacing GDP: Allianz Research
articles.summary: India’s insurance industry is poised for sustained expansion over the next decade, supported by structural drivers such as ageing demographics, rising healthcare needs and widening protection gaps. While recent performance shows steady premium growth across life, health and general insurance segments, the sector continues to face significant underpenetration challenges despite its size and rapid development.

Shah also highlighted long-term opportunities in sectors linked to energy security, electric vehicles, defence, AI investments and data centres.

articles.title: Malviya Nagar fire incident: MEA extends assistance, in touch with embassies
articles.summary: At least 21 people were killed and several others injured after a massive fire engulfed a B B hotel in Delhi's Malviya Nagar on Wednesday. Firefighters rescued 37 occupants, while 39 victims were taken to Max Hospital, where 18 were declared dead and 15 remain in intensive care. Authorities said the hotel lacked a mandatory fire NOC, and police have launched a manhunt for the owner while investigating possible safety violations. Foreign nationals accounted for 17 of the 21 fatalities. Prime Mini

This event is seen as a boost to India's tiger conservation efforts under existing wildlife protection policies.

articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

articles.summary: The online movement 'Cockroach Janata Party' has named three spokespersons—Saurav Das, Vijeta Dahiya, and Ashutosh Ranka—who will represent the group ahead of a planned protest on June 6. The movement aims to amplify its demands through these designated voices. Protests often seek policy changes or government action, making this relevant to India's political and civil liberties landscape.

articles.title: When justice needs a headline
articles.summary: This article by Mahima Kaushik, a mental wellness specialist from Namah Foundation, examines systemic challenges in India's justice system. It explores how delays and inefficiencies in legal processes impact vulnerable groups, particularly those with mental health concerns. The piece suggests these issues may necessitate broader policy reforms to improve access to justice and mental health support within the legal framework.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Mahima Kaushik, mental wellness specialist, Namah Foundation.

articles.title: Empowering the street vendors: 6 years of PM SVANidhi
articles.summary: The article evaluates the six-year performance of the PM SVANidhi scheme, a government initiative launched to support street vendors in India. Authored by Ashok Chandra, MD & CEO of Punjab National Bank, it highlights the scheme's impact on empowering street vendors. The PM SVANidhi scheme provides collateral-free loans to street vendors to help them resume or expand their businesses. The article assesses the scheme's effectiveness and outcomes over the past six years.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Ashok Chandra, MD & CEO, Punjab National Bank.

articles.title: Pakistan and Iran: An awkward tango at play

articles.title: Must end ‘stale, frozen’ marriages: SC urges judiciary to end prolonged marital disputes
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) urged the judiciary to resolve prolonged marital disputes, stating that keeping 'dead marriages' alive through years of litigation causes emotional distress. A bench of justices highlighted that such delays perpetuate frustration and called for systemic changes to end these prolonged disputes. The SC's directive aims to address the emotional and legal burdens on individuals trapped in stale marriages.

articles.title: Gujarat: 362 illegal Bangladeshi nationals held in statewide crackdown

articles.title: Strong El Nino developing, South Asia to get below normal rain, flags WMO
articles.summary: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned that a strong El Nino is developing, which could result in below-normal rainfall across much of South Asia during the June–September 2026 monsoon season. This forecast raises concerns about potential impacts on agriculture, water resources, and livelihoods in the region, including India. The WMO's alert highlights the need for preparedness and mitigation measures.
articles.raw_summary: Rainfall is likely to be below normal during the June–September 2026 southwest monsoon season across much of South Asia

articles.title: China’s high-tech rise is leaving much of the country behind
articles.summary: China's rapid advancement in high technology is exacerbating regional inequalities within the country, leaving many behind. This trend highlights growing disparities in access to opportunities and resources. The situation may prompt discussions on policy measures to address economic and technological divides, which could have broader implications for countries like India navigating similar challenges.
articles.raw_summary: That could make a starkly unequal country even more so

The situation highlights potential vulnerabilities in India's energy supply chain and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Oil prices rose for a third day on Wednesday and the dollar was on the brink of breaking the 160 yen barrier as fresh hostilities flared in the Gulf.
